analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet announces that newly approved COVID-19 vaccines are being shipped to all 27 EU countries for coordinated EU Vaccination Days on 27–29 December. Evaluation: EU1 (EU is a successful story): Implicitly suggests effective action and organization, but it does not reference history or overcoming past traumas; focus is present logistics, so weak fit. EU2 (EU is united): Strong fit—explicit reference to dispatching vaccines to all 27 EU countries and a coordinated vaccination schedule highlights unity and solidarity in action. EU3 (EU is useful for its citizens): Strong fit—the EU is organizing vaccine distribution and a synchronized rollout, demonstrating practical, timely action improving public health for EU citizens. EU4 (avant-garde actor): No claim of pioneering leadership or regulation; not supported. EU5 (global champion of fair causes): Not addressed. EU6 (contributes to a better world): The scope is internal to the EU; no global angle. Conclusion: The tweet promotes EU2 and EU3.
